selected_important_turn.text: Okay. Lets just try to do some negotiation. I am the most interested in getting more WATER. The water I already have at the campsite looks dirty so, if it is okay with you, I would like all THREE of them. My food has also been stolen by bears and I don't make much left, so I would like TWO of the food items. You can have all the firewood and the other food item. How does that sound? 🙂
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete allocation from YOU and it shifts the exchange from a cooperative, idea-focused stance into a competitive, zero-sum framing by claiming all three waters and most food. It anchors the division and defines priorities in a self-favoring way, setting the tone for subsequent give-and-take.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although it communicates real needs, it's rigid and claim-heavy (taking all three waters) rather than inviting trade-offs or asking about the opponent's priorities. Coming off the opponent's prosocial suggestion, this abrupt move missed a clear opportunity to build integrative value (e.g., by proposing trades, explaining why water matters, or asking about the other's best alternatives). The strong anchor risks reducing joint gains and harming rapport.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Keep the clear expression of needs (water priority) but avoid claiming all units as an immediate demand. Instead: (a) justify need briefly, (b) ask for the opponent's priorities, and (c) propose one or two integrative packages (e.g., I take 2 water + 1 food in exchange for you taking all firewood, or I take 3 water if you take an extra food or something I value less). Frame as options and ask if they'd consider tradeoffs rather than issuing a full claim.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase — a trade-oriented wording would open integrative trade possibilities rather than locking a zero-sum split
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — offering options and trading signals concern for mutual fairness rather than seizing all of one good
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — softer anchoring and explicit trade proposals make it easier to find acceptable packages
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — asking and proposing rather than demanding reduces friction and builds trust

value2reason.Low: To outgrow the size of limitation in order to satisfy my deficiencies.
value2reason.Medium: To own excess so that it will surely result in even more security
value2reason.High: To feel better and move closer to pleasure.

value2reason.Low: The firewood I brought got rained on and it won't catch on fire.
value2reason.Medium: A bear has broken into my camp and stole all our food!
value2reason.High: The water at the camping site looks like it may be polluted and I need fresh water.

dialogue.text: i suggest we put excess in charge of working on proposition because prevention of excess is not a good first step.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I don't know what you mean by "prevention of excess." Can you clarify?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: what i mean by prevention of excess is that we should focus on getting excess and not to limit it regardless of what we already have because if we should offer some creative approaches by excess. we can both come to an agreement if you are interested.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Okay. Lets just try to do some negotiation. I am the most interested in getting more WATER. The water I already have at the campsite looks dirty so, if it is okay with you, I would like all THREE of them. My food has also been stolen by bears and I don't make much left, so I would like TWO of the food items. You can have all the firewood and the other food item. How does that sound? 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate your interest in water, but I value food more. Let's consider a trade: I'll give you one food package for one water package. That way, you get more water, and I keep the rest of the food. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your perspective, but I need more water than food. What if I take 2 water and 1 food, and you take the remaining 1 water and 2 food? That way, I get more of what I need, and you still get some food. How does that work for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m willing to trade one food for one water, but I need two food packages for the three water you want. Let’s split the firewood evenly—two each. That way, I get more food, and you get enough water. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need more water than food, but I’m open to trade. How about I take 2 water and 1 food, and you take 1 water and 2 food? We can split the firewood evenly. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m open to that split. Let’s confirm: you take 2 water, 1 food, and 2 firewood; I take 1 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood. That way, you get more water, and I get more food. Deal?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
